howItWorks.intro: This dps calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How is DPS calculated?
faq.answer: Base DPS = Damage × Attack Speed. With crits: Effective DPS = Base DPS × (1 + Crit Chance × (Crit Multiplier - 1)). This gives average damage over time including critical hits.

faq.question: Is the DPS Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What damage per hit should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate damage per hit value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the DPS Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

sections.title: What Is the DPS Calculator?
sections.body: The DPS Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your damage per hit, attacks per second, crit chance, you get instant results including base dps, effective dps, dps from crits. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting base dps right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your damage per hit and need to find the right base dps. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Damage per Hit
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your damage per hit — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: attacks per second, crit chance, crit damage multiplier. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your base dps, effective dps, dps from crits. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your damage per hit before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both base dps and effective dps — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your crit damage multiplier, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
